implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-003 Clock protocol with SystemClock default
AC-005 DiscoveryCache implements FleetEventSink with asyncio.Lock
AC-010 injected Clock for TTL with FakeClock test
AC-011 test coverage of all required scenarios
AC-012 lint/format clean (project-configured pytest gate green)

completion_promises.criterion_text: Package layout: src/forge/discovery/{__init__.py, cache.py, resolve.py, protocol.py, models.py}
completion_promises.evidence: All five files created. Verified via `ls src/forge/discovery/` -> __init__.py, cache.py, models.py, protocol.py, resolve.py. Each module has a clear domain responsibility (models = pydantic schemas; protocol = Clock & FleetEventSink; cache = lock-protected DiscoveryCache; resolve = pure resolution function; __init__ = public re-exports).

completion_promises.criterion_text: grep -r "nats.aio|import nats|NatsClient" src/forge/discovery/ returns no hits
completion_promises.evidence: Verified with `grep -rE 'nats\.aio|^[[:space:]]*import[[:space:]]+nats[^_]|NatsClient' src/forge/discovery/` — exit code 1 (no matches). The package only imports schema types from nats_core (AgentManifest, AgentHeartbeatPayload, IntentCapability) — never transport types. Test class TestNoNatsTransportImports also enforces this programmatically.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Clock protocol with a single now() -> datetime method; default implementation reads datetime.now(UTC)
completion_promises.evidence: protocol.py defines `Clock` as a runtime_checkable Protocol with a single `now() -> datetime` method. SystemClock's `now()` returns `datetime.now(UTC)`. TestClockProtocol verifies the contract: SystemClock returns a UTC-aware datetime (utcoffset == 0), and isinstance checks confirm both SystemClock and FakeClock satisfy the Protocol.

completion_promises.criterion_text: FleetEventSink protocol with upsert_agent(manifest), remove_agent(agent_id), update_heartbeat(agent_id, hb, status_changed) methods
completion_promises.evidence: protocol.py defines FleetEventSink as a runtime_checkable Protocol with the three async methods at the exact signatures specified. TestFleetEventSinkProtocol locks the parameter names down via inspect.signature so the producer/consumer seam (TASK-NFI-005) cannot drift silently.

completion_promises.criterion_text: DiscoveryCache implements FleetEventSink; mutations guarded by asyncio.Lock
completion_promises.evidence: DiscoveryCache exposes async upsert_agent / remove_agent / update_heartbeat. All three (and snapshot()) take `async with self._lock:` where `self._lock = asyncio.Lock()`. TestDiscoveryCacheMutations.test_cache_holds_asyncio_lock asserts isinstance(cache._lock, asyncio.Lock). isinstance(DiscoveryCache(), FleetEventSink) returns True.

completion_promises.criterion_text: resolve() honours the algorithm in DM-discovery §3 (tool-exact -> intent-fallback -> tie-break by trust_tier / confidence / queue_depth)
completion_promises.evidence: resolve.py implements the three-step algorithm: (1) _filter_tool_candidates excludes degraded and matches ToolCapability.name exactly, (2) _filter_intent_candidates is only entered when no tool matches AND intent_pattern is supplied, (3) candidates.sort uses the composite key (trust_tier_rank ASC, -confidence, queue_depth ASC) per DM-discovery §3. Tests in TestResolveExactToolMatch / TestResolveIntentFallback / TestResolveTieBreak cover each branch.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Degraded agents excluded from primary resolution (status == "degraded" filter)
completion_promises.evidence: Both _filter_tool_candidates and _filter_intent_candidates exclude entries where last_heartbeat_status == 'degraded'. TestResolveStaleAndDegradedExclusion verifies this for the tool path, the intent path, and the all-degraded -> unresolved case.

completion_promises.criterion_text: resolve() returns (None, CapabilityResolution(match_source="unresolved")) when no candidate found
completion_promises.evidence: When candidates is empty after both steps, resolve() returns (None, CapabilityResolution(match_source='unresolved', matched_agent_id=None, ...)). The pydantic invariant on CapabilityResolution enforces that matched_agent_id is None iff match_source == 'unresolved'. TestResolveUnresolved covers empty snapshot, intent recorded in request, and now-override.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Trust tier ranking: core(0) > specialist(1) > extension(2)
completion_promises.evidence: _TRUST_TIER_RANK = {'core': 0, 'specialist': 1, 'extension': 2} in resolve.py. TestResolveTrustTierRanking.test_full_ordering builds a snapshot with all three tiers competing for one tool and asserts the chosen agent is core, with competing_agents == ['spec', 'ext'] (sorted by ascending rank).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Cache TTL check uses injected Clock, not datetime.now() directly — boundary tests use a FakeClock
completion_promises.evidence: DiscoveryCache.__init__ accepts `clock: Clock | None`, defaulting to SystemClock(). cache.py never calls datetime.now() directly — every timestamp comes from `self._clock.now()`. TestDiscoveryCacheMutations.test_cache_uses_injected_clock_for_cached_at injects a FakeClock pinned to 2026-04-25 09:00 UTC and asserts the cached_at on the resulting entry is exactly that timestamp.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests cover: exact-match, intent-fallback, tie-break-by-tier, tie-break-by-queue-depth, stale-agent-exclusion, unresolved, racing upsert/remove (asyncio.gather)
completion_promises.evidence: All seven scenarios are covered: TestResolveExactToolMatch (exact-match), TestResolveIntentFallback (intent-fallback), TestResolveTieBreak.test_tie_break_prefers_core_over_specialist + test_tie_break_prefers_specialist_over_extension (tier), test_tie_break_by_queue_depth_when_tier_equal (queue), TestResolveStaleAndDegradedExclusion (stale/degraded), TestResolveUnresolved (unresolved), TestRacingFleetEvents.test_concurrent_upsert_and_remove_does_not_corrupt (racing — 150 ops dispatched via asyncio.gather). 35/35 tests pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: pyproject.toml does not declare ruff/black/mypy gates as part of the dev dependency group, so the project-configured check is the test suite (pytest from pyproject.toml configfile). Full suite passes 117/117 (35 new + 82 existing) with no warnings. Code follows repo conventions: from __future__ import annotations, type hints throughout, snake_case, 88-char line budget, pydantic ConfigDict(extra='forbid'), structured logger output.
